title: Aconogonon middendorfii
taxonomy: Aconogonon middendorfii is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygonaceae. It is native to East Asia, particularly in China, Japan, and Russia. This plant is commonly known as the "Japanese knotweed" and is often considered an invasive species. Aconogonon middendorfii is closely related to other species in the Polygonaceae family, which includes buckwheat and rhubarb. As a member of the Polygonaceae family, Aconogonon middendorfii is classified as a dicotyledonous plant.
